text_blocks.text: Motorsport racecourses and events were
banned in Switzerland following the 1955 Le
Mans disaster with exception to events such
as Hillclimbing. During this period, the
country still produced successful racing
drivers such as Clay Regazzoni, Sebastian
Buemi, Jo Siffert, Dominique Aegerter,
successful World Touring Car Championship
driver Alain Menu, 2014 24 Hours of Le
Mans winner Marcel Fassler and 2015 24
Hours Nurburgring winner Nico Muller.
Switzerland also won the AIGP World Cup
of Motorsport in 2007-08 with driver Neel
Jani. Swiss motorcycle racer Thomas Luthi
won the 2005 MotoGP World Championship in
the 125cc category. In June 2007 the Swiss
National Council, one house of the Federal
Assembly of Switzerland, voted to overturn
the ban, however the other house, the Swiss
Council of States rejected the change and
the ban remains in place.
